note_data.title: People in Business
keyPoints: **Motivation** is the reason why employees want to work well and work effectively for the business
**Financial motivation** includes piece rate, commission, profit sharing, and performance-related pay to encourage higher productivity
**Non-financial motivation** includes job rotation, job enlargement, job enrichment, and team working to increase job satisfaction
**Recruitment** is the process of identifying that the business needs to employ someone up to the point where applications have arrived at the business
**Selection** is the process of interviewing and choosing the best candidate for the job from those who have applied
**Internal recruitment** involves appointing existing employees to new posts within the business, which saves costs and motivates staff
**External recruitment** involves appointing people from outside the business, which brings new ideas and skills
**Training** improves employee performance and includes induction training, on-the-job training, and off-the-job training
**Job description** outlines the tasks, duties and responsibilities of the job
**Person specification** outlines the qualifications, skills and experience needed to do the job effectively

definitions.definition: The reason why employees want to work well and work effectively for the business
The process of identifying that the business needs to employ someone up to the point where applications have arrived at the business
The process of teaching employees the skills needed to do their job effectively
A document that outlines the tasks, duties and responsibilities of a particular job
Giving a subordinate the authority to perform particular tasks

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Name or select from given information. No explanation required.
Give main features or general principles. Brief explanation needed.

modelAnswer.question: Explain two ways a business could motivate its employees. [6 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: One way is to offer performance-related pay. This means employees receive extra payment based on how well they perform their job. This motivates employees because they have a financial incentive to work harder and more effectively. Another way is job enlargement. This means giving employees more tasks of a similar level to make their job more varied. This motivates employees because it makes their work less boring and repetitive, increasing job satisfaction.
markBreakdown: Mark 3: Explanation of why it motivates
Mark 6: Explanation of why it motivates

commonMistakes: Never say 'money always motivates' — some employees are motivated by non-financial factors like recognition
Students often confuse job enlargement (more tasks same level) with job enrichment (more challenging tasks)
Don't just list recruitment stages — explain why each stage is important for selecting the right candidate
Never say training is 'expensive waste' — always explain benefits like increased productivity and reduced accidents
Students often forget that internal recruitment creates another vacancy that needs filling
examinerTips: This topic appears in almost every exam paper - learn all motivation theories thoroughly
Always give specific examples when explaining motivation methods
For recruitment questions, mention both advantages AND disadvantages of internal/external recruitment
Training questions often ask about costs vs benefits - prepare both sides
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Maslow's hierarchy of needs pyramid showing physiological needs at bottom, then safety, social, esteem, and self-actualization at top. Essential for explaining different motivation factors.
